_doc: Live current-affairs signals for content:research. Claude refreshes this each batch (Step 0) via WebSearch + LinkedIn-trend check, then the command merges it: a topic whose keyword matches a signal takes the verified headline as its title and the verified fact as its Notes (real copy, not a flag), plus a rank boost. This is what makes posts current-affairs-grounded instead of stale-bank. Verify + re-date every batch — rules and dates move.

signals.headline: ETIAS starts Q4 2026: a EUR 20 travel authorisation for UK passports, not a visa
signals.fact: ETIAS is an online travel authorisation for visa-exempt UK passport holders. EUR 20, valid 3 years, launching Oct-Dec 2026 with a 6-month grace period before it is mandatory (into ~April 2027). It is not a visa. Only the official EU site is legitimate; anyone selling it now is a scam.
signals.trend: LinkedIn: heavily posted by ABTA, Grayling, national interior ministries as an authority explainer

signals.headline: EES is already live: the biometric Schengen border replaced passport stamps on 10 April 2026
signals.fact: The EU Entry/Exit System became fully operational across all 29 Schengen countries on 10 April 2026 (progressive rollout began 12 October 2025). Fingerprints and a facial scan on first entry; over 45 million crossings logged. No traveller sign-up, but expect longer queues while it beds in.
signals.trend: LinkedIn: ABTA + travel associations posting timeline explainers
